summary: Add authentication gate to Age of Apes app by updating its layout and registry entry, with smoke tests.
implementation:
1. apps/web/lib/app-registry.ts: Change `auth: false` to `auth: true` in the age-of-apes entry.
2. apps/web/app/apps/age-of-apes/layout.tsx: Import `requireAppLayoutAccess` from `@/lib/require-app-layout-access`. Make the default export async. Add `await requireAppLayoutAccess("age-of-apes")` as the first statement in the function body.
3. apps/web/app/apps/age-of-apes/__tests__/layout.test.tsx: Create test file following the Area 52 pattern. Mock `@/lib/require-app-layout-access`. Test four cases: (a) calls requireAppLayoutAccess with 'age-of-apes', (b) unauthenticated user gets NEXT_REDIRECT to /login, (c) unauthorized user gets NEXT_REDIRECT to /unauthorized, (d) authenticated user with permission renders children.

testing.reason: Auth gating changes runtime behavior—unauthenticated/unauthorized users must be redirected. Smoke tests verify the layout calls requireAppLayoutAccess correctly and propagates redirect errors.

verification.reason: Auth gating is server-side redirect logic fully covered by unit tests. No new UI elements to visually verify.
